profile.name: Iván
profile.title: VP of Engineering @ JOOR
profile.description:
It all started with War Games; I watched Matthew Broderick hacking a phone, and it looked reeeeeeally cool.

So I first started breaking stuff (I called it research). Then I got my Spectrum 48k at 6, and... voila, suddenly I knew what I wanted to do for a living.

I studied for a bachelor's and master's in **Computer Science** at the University of Oviedo and then started working.

After **20 years in the business**, I've done pretty much everything: research, **data analysis**, consulting, freelancing as a software architect, **building a company** from scratch, and, lastly, shaping remote and efficient engineering organizations **(30 to 50 people)**.

Now, I focus on getting the optimal balance between devs happiness & business efficiency by making things as lean as possible.

I still dedicate 20% of my time to coding, to stay technically relevant and support my engineering team better, but finally, because I genuinely enjoy it. 

Next?
Joining a purposeful org made of great and talented human beings and working in reasonable "zero shit" contexts, ideally as a hands-on lead.

challenges.description:
As VP of Engineering, my role is centered on scaling impact, not distance from the work.

I aim to stay close to technical conversations so I can lead by example, informed judgment, and real context rather than abstraction. This allows me to guide architectural decisions, platform direction, and engineering standards with credibility and pragmatism.

Beyond engineering, I’m intentionally broadening my influence across the organization—partnering with product, operations, and leadership to extend strong engineering practices into how we plan, execute, and measure work company-wide.

Key focus areas for the role include:

 - Consolidating and modernizing core platform initiatives to reduce systemic risk, improve resilience, and create a foundation for long-term scalability.

 - Defining and driving an AI strategy that transforms our SDLC end-to-end—from discovery and design to delivery, operations, and learning—far beyond code assistance.

 - Shaping an execution model that favors clarity, ownership, and outcomes over process density, as teams and scope continue to grow.

My goal as VP is to ensure engineering remains a strategic advantage for the company: technically excellent, operationally efficient, and deeply aligned with business outcomes.

I helped Haliooo Ventures build their first initiative with https://deepmimic.ai.

This platform allows businesses to create simulated markets, synthetic focus groups, and people to spot market signals, validate strategies, messages, pricing, ... prior to the real launch.

As a fractional tech advisor, I acted mostly as a senior IC, from design to programming and deploying.

The platform was built using DDD on top of NextJS, Typescript, Clerk, FastAPI, Postgres and served via Kubernetes in AWS.

As Senior Director of Engineering, I led a globally distributed team of 30+ engineers across multiple time zones, operating with an async-first mindset and a lean, pragmatic approach to the SDLC.

My focus has been on scaling impact rather than process: optimizing how teams work, how systems perform, and how the organization makes decisions. Key outcomes include:

 - Driving a **cost-efficiency** initiative that reduced AWS spend by over $200K while achieving 99.99% availability and improving platform **performance** by 15%.

 - Reinforcing an **async-first, low-ceremony operating model** that enabled high autonomy and consistent delivery across geographies.

 - Serving on an internal executive task force to **audit company operations** and propose structural and process changes to improve overall efficiency.

 - Leading the company’s initial **adoption of AI**, from accelerating development cycles to shipping the first major AI-powered customer features into production.

Throughout this role, I’ve stayed close to both the technical and organizational layers, balancing system-level thinking with hands-on leadership to ensure we deliver measurable business outcomes without sacrificing engineering quality.

Managing a tribe made of exceptional engineers while building the world´s premier wholesale management system.

For the past two and a half years, I've served as a director of engineering, overseeing a transformation within our engineering department. Initially, the team comprised approximately 50 engineers, with 30 being consultants. Recognizing the low quality of the code and high costs, I initiated a comprehensive overhaul to improve our efficiency and deliverability.

Key achievements include:

 - Leading a cultural shift towards asynchronous communication, critical minimal documentation, and the elimination of low-value agile ceremonies.
 - Streamlining the team to 35 members while enhancing productivity, resulting in significantly improved code quality.
 - Boosting CI/CD performance by 50% and reducing infrastructure costs by 50%.
 - Achieving high retention, with only three team members leaving since the transformation began.

Currently, I manage three engineering managers, two tech leads, and two staff engineers. I remain technically engaged, contributing to critical technical decisions and dedicating 15% of my time to hands-on coding (js, python, or even small legacy php bits if needed), ensuring I stay connected with the team's day-to-day challenges.

For almost two years, I've been the Head of Engineering, leading a diverse team of up to 15 engineers from Ukraine, Belarus, Israel, and primarily Spain. My main responsibility has been overseeing the technical integration of Sweetspot Intelligence, a Spain-based startup specializing in data visualization with RoR and BigQuery, into the data systems of ClickDimensions.

Key achievements include:

 - Successfully leading the integration of Sweetspot Intelligence's product into ClickDimensions' data systems.
 - Refactoring data pipelines using a combination of Azure Data Factory and PySpark, resulting in a 90% reduction in solution costs.
 - Managing a geographically dispersed team while maintaining high levels of collaboration and productivity by redefining the SDLC processes.

I was the technical co-founder of Zapiens, initially working part-time with just 2.5 people on the team. As the idea gained traction, I transitioned to full-time and progressively built an engineering team of up to 15 members.

Key achievements include:

 - Single-handedly developing the first version of the solution, which included a hybrid mobile app (available on Android, iOS, and even Blackberry) built with Ionic, a Spring Boot API, an Angular-based web admin site, and a MySQL database.
 - Evolving the original solution by dockerizing it for deployment in, both, shared AWS and private clouds for specific customers.
 - Introducing a **custom chatbot**, leveraging Python and Elasticsearch, before GPT models and during the early days of BERT.
 - Taking on the role of COO for several months, gaining exposure to support, sales, product, and marketing challenges, as the **company grew to 45 employees**.
 - Helping the company achieve around 10k recurring monthly users and nearly €1M in ARR.
 - Securing €1.5M in funding from the EU and establishing Zapiens' first partnership with Microsoft before my departure.

I was hired as a freelance software engineer by EyeSeeTea (https://eyeseetea.com/). As their first hire, I joined the three founders in building solutions to address infectious disease containment in third-world countries.

Key achievements include:

 - Developing a couple of Android native mobile apps in collaboration with the founders.
 - Utilizing DHIS2 (https://dhis2.org/) as the backend solution for these apps.
 - Localizing the apps into several languages to enhance accessibility and usability in diverse regions.

Periodicis aimed to create real-time relationships between news from various sources, enabling users to follow stories from multiple perspectives and simplifying professionals' tasks.

As a founder, I:
 - Developed the startup's vision and business plan.
 - Engaged with potential investors and made critical decisions.

As an engineer, I created a native JavaScript extension for the Chrome browser that:
 - Detected when a user was on a 'news' page.
 - Extracted key information and displayed a real-time carousel of related news from other sources.

The project was selected twice as a Wayra finalist, Spain's leading accelerator, among hundreds of projects, and received excellent feedback.

If curious, here’s a presentation of Periodicis at the Innovation Convention in Brussels in 2011: Video.

Nortia was a small startup that developed software products to help large companies make data-driven decisions based on metrics rather than opinions.

As the team leader of a small group of engineers (4 people), I:
 - Defined the product roadmap in collaboration with one of the founders.
 - Designed and implemented clear approaches to tackle programming challenges.
 - Promoted best practices in testing, designing, and programming within the team.

Our main product, Segmentia, was a marketing automation tool that enabled non-technical users to find and communicate with segments of entities (customers, providers, etc.) within any datamart using a visual interface. Key achievements included:
 - Creating a full abstraction of the database and its drivers by developing an intermediate DSL that dynamically transformed into different SQL flavors.
 - Utilizing Grails for the backend, Griffon for the frontend, and a Groovy engine that connected both via RMI, with Gradle for build automation.
 - Deploying the software at several major clients, running on diverse datamarts with millions of rows.

Additionally, we developed other products, such as:
 - A POS-CRM: A Grails + Redis web app that provided real-time client information to the POS systems of a major supermarket chain via a REST API.
 - Konfides: A Grails-based solution for SMEs to engage clients with personalized communications and offers, built on top of a Drools rules engine.

IECISA (Informática el Corte Inglés, S.A.) is the largest Spanish consulting firm, specializing in consulting services and outsourcing solutions.

After serving as the regional eBusiness manager, I was promoted to software factory manager for the eBusiness division. In this role, I:

 - Managed a team of 25-30 people, overseeing a remote software factory that supported numerous projects across Spain (IECISA , Inves).
 - Promoted the adoption of agile practices within the team, reviewed workflows, and established a set of good practices to enhance efficiency.
 - Supervised projects primarily involving portal solutions, such as Vignette (now OpenText) and Liferay, for which I actively worked to secure a partnership in Spain.

As a regional manager, I began with a team of 4 people, which grew to 10 as we earned customer trust by delivering projects on time despite challenging circumstances.

This role taught me the importance of meticulous attention to detail, including:
 - Creating accurate estimations and following up with customers.
 - Choosing the right technical approaches.
 - Managing team motivation, addressing issues, and handling all related responsibilities.

We worked with both private and public clients, with a notable specialization in eGovernment. Some of our projects are still operational today (https://www.asturias.es/, https://www.astursalud.es/).

Neo Metrics (now acquired by Accenture and renamed Accenture Analytics) was the first Spanish firm specializing in consulting analytics. During my time there, the company grew from 30 to 100 employees, providing me with the opportunity to navigate rapidly changing environments and occasional chaos.

As a project manager, I:

 - Led a team of 4 engineers to develop a Web 2.0 central repository of commercial actions for Spain’s largest bank, which operates globally.
 - Faced challenges including tight deadlines, a steep learning curve, and mastering a full stack (Spring MVC).

As a software engineer, I collaborated with data scientists to develop processes and applications that helped customers interpret their extensive data. I played a key engineering role in several notable projects, including:

 - Optimizing Advertisement Scheduling: Developed a system for a TV channel to find the optimal schedule for advertisements while meeting numerous constraints. This involved solving a system of 300,000 inequations using Ilog (a commercial mathematical library), managing JVM Garbage Collector issues, consuming SOAP services via XFire, and providing a remote API via RMI.

 - Social Network Analysis Prototype: Built a prototype for a Spanish telco using Eclipse RCP, enabling the examination of customer relationships as a graph (using JUNG) to forecast behavior when a community member leaves.

 - Custom Struts-based Pipeline Dashboard: Designed and programmed a custom dashboard for a leading media group. This project included developing the web application, designing the datamart, and creating ETL processes (using PL/SQL) to generate forecasts and metrics for billing and income projections.

 - Other Projects: Worked on various smaller yet impactful projects using SAS.

organization.name: API (Project Area Engineering) - University of Oviedo

challenges.description:
My first working experience was as a software engineer intern at the University of Oviedo's research lab ([API Uniovi](http://www.api.uniovi.es)).

During this internship, I contributed to two projects:

- **KM4SME (Knowledge Management for Small and Medium Enterprises)**: Developed an interactive web-based knowledge management model for SMEs, funded by the European Union, using SharePoint and .NET.

- **Data Mining Application**: Created an application for a German factory to automatically optimize cutting widths in their production process, using .NET and DLLs.

details.name: Deepmimic
details.description:
I served as a tech advisor and architect for this side project, programming key features in my free time.

Powered by synthetic personas and simulated markets, the system spots market signals and validates strategy, messaging, design, and pricing prior to launch.

It is built using a custom multi-agent solution on top of a NextJS + FastAPI stack.

details.name: Malariapp
details.description: QoC Android App - Open-source quality assessments tool connected with DHIS2 Health Information Servers. Helps with health center assessments, featuring automatic survey generation and data sync with DHIS2

details.name: Pictureapp
details.description: QIS Malaria Surveillance - Open-source mobile application designed to report positive cases of **malaria detected in Cambodia** in near real-time to a DHIS2 server. Used by healthcare providers in the field after mRDT tests.

manfredSpecificData.desiredJobDescription:
My dream job is based on 3 main features:
 - Purpose: it must have a meaningful or, at least, honest purpose. Better ads, Bet houses ... are not.
 - People: it must be a place for sharing my experience and learn from others (I love learning). No rockstars or superstars inside, just lovely people to give and take.
 - Perks: reasonable salary for a seasoned father of 2 (>50k), remote friendly (I will work from Asturias)

Regarding my duties, It depends on the challenge. I've been the manager, the CTO, the head of engineering, the project manager, the consultant, the data engineer ... but I mostly consider myself a software engineer with good communication skills.

Lastly, in terms of tech, I feel comfortable working with lots of different technologies like java, js, sql, aws, elasticsearch but I dont really think this is important per se. I'm much more inclined to focus on strong backgrounds and the hability to learn whatever is needed.

PD: Please keep this guy away from css, ;)
manfredSpecificData.goodPractices:
Generalist, curious, and pragmatic.
My principles:
 - People first
 - Make things simple
 - Keep moving forward
 - Decouple and compose 😉
